excerpt: Adam's forehead was fashioned from the clay of the Sacred House itself, his ankles from Egyptian soil, his feet from the clay of Hijaz, his right hand from the clay of the East, and his left hand from the clay of the West.
source_location_hint: Early Islamic sources on Adam's creation

excerpt: I was a prophet when Ādam was still in the state of water and clay.
source_location_hint: Ḥadīth on the precedence of Muhammad

excerpt: This greeting is a blessing and a gift for all believers born from you and your descendants until the Day of Resurrection.
source_location_hint: Commentary on the greeting of peace

excerpt: The angels' utterance—their proclamation that they alone sufficed for the praise of the Divine—constituted what was made manifest. What remained concealed was their inner thought: how could the Almighty create a being superior to themselves.
source_work: Kirmani, al-Riyad or related tafsīr commentary

excerpt: When God shaped Adam's form from clay and positioned it along the path the angels traversed, they marvelled at the beauty of that figure. When Iblīs passed by, he examined the lifeless form and found it hollow within, and spoke disparagingly to the angels about it.
source_work: al-Tafsīr al-Bāṭinī or Kirmani, al-Masābih fī Ithbāt al-Imāma

excerpt: God comprehended both the disobedience hidden in the angels' hearts and the obstinate defiance that Iblīs concealed within his breast. Nothing of their inner states escaped Divine knowledge.
...since Adam did not have intestines in his body, the digestive power fruit was forbidden so that they would not eat it, and then the fruit would rot in his stomach...
source_location_hint: Section on Ādam's Expulsion, physiological interpretation

excerpt: These sciences are unveiled to those worthy of them at the appointed hour, according to divine wisdom.
source_location_hint: Doctrine of esoteric sciences and progressive revelation

excerpt: In every epoch there appears an Ādam, and that Ādam errs within the lower veil; yet his higher nature remains immaculate among the purified.
source_location_hint: Doctrine of prophetic immunity (ʿiṣmah) and dual veiling

excerpt: Through these seven words (utterances), Ādam repented to his Lord—the first Munbaʿith—and his repentance was accepted, restoring him to Paradise (the ranks of intellect).
source_work: Al-Sijistani, Kitab al-Yanabiʿ (on the seven emanations and intellects)
source_location_hint: Cosmological ladder of intellects and repentance

excerpt: Those words were none other than the blessed names of Muhammad, ʿAlī, Fāṭimah, al-Ḥasan, and al-Ḥusayn, upon them be peace.
source_authority: Ismaili teaching on the Panjtan Pak (Five Pure Ones)

excerpt: Through these holy names, he repented, saying to the Imām: 'We beseech forgiveness from You through these blessed names!' The Imām accepted Ādam's repentance and forgave him.
source_work: Qāḍī al-Nūʿmān, Taʾwil al-Daʿaʾim (on intercession and repentance)
source_location_hint: Chapter on repentance and the role of the saints

excerpt: He embodies the opening on the days of the dīn (religion), and he is the symbolic embodiment of wilāyah in the Dāʿim al-Islām (the Enduring Commandments of Islam).
source_location_hint: Doctrine of Ādam and the principles of wilāyah

excerpt: The Sharīʿah of Muḥammad, destined to emerge after six thousand years, was founded and established by him.
source_location_hint: Doctrine of temporal cycles and foundational prophecies
